svg.text: HOW PLANTS MAKE THEIR OWN FOOD
Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants convert sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide into glucose and oxygen.

g.text: ➤ Water molecules split (photolysis): H₂O → H⁺ + O₂
➤ Oxygen is released as a by-product
➤ CO₂ is fixed by RuBisCO enzyme
➤ ATP and NADPH reduce carbon compounds
Air spaces for gas exchange; some photosynthesis
Tiny pores: CO₂ enters, O₂ and H₂O exit
Green pigment that absorbs red and blue light; reflects green
Tiny pores on leaf underside; CO₂ in, O₂ and water vapour out
Energy (respiration), growth (cellulose), or stored as starch
Photosynthesis = only in light; Respiration = all the time
Different plants use different pathways based on climate
Most abundant protein on Earth; catalyzes carbon fixation
